content: Retrieval-augmented context management can fix a critical underaddressed flaw in 2025-era ICM curiosity-driven autonomous agents: ICM incentivizes agents to seek out novel, unvisited states, but it does not provide a structured way to avoid re-exploring already mapped low-reward novelty while retaining high-fidelity memory of high-value unexpected discoveries. By tying ICM's intrinsic novelty score to a retrieval-augmented memory bank that prioritizes storing and retrieving only high-novelty, high-generalizable states, agents can sustain long-horizon exploration without catastrophic forgetting of useful insights or wasting compute on redundant novelty-seeking.
